findings.message: Destructive Delete action has no confirmation step. A misclick irreversibly removes the item with no undo, which is a confusing/dangerous flow for an irreversible action.
findings.suggestion: Gate the delete behind a confirmation (e.g. window.confirm or a confirm dialog/modal), or implement an undo affordance after deletion.

findings.message: Mutating action (DELETE) provides no feedback: the button is not disabled while the request is in flight, and there is no success or error indicator. On slow networks users may click repeatedly; on failure they get no signal that the action did not succeed.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state (e.g. useState for isDeleting), disable the button while pending, and surface success/error feedback (toast, inline message, or role=alert) instead of relying on window.location.reload().

findings.message: Using window.location.reload() as the success path is a dead-end/jarring flow: it discards client state, shows a full page reload, and hides any error from a failed DELETE (the await result is not checked, so a non-2xx response silently still reloads).
findings.suggestion: Check the response.ok of the DELETE call, update local items state on success (optimistic or post-response), and render an error message on failure rather than unconditionally reloading the page.
